## Systems Involved

| System | Role | Integration Method |
|--------|------|-------------------|
| [System A] | Source of truth for [data type] | API / Webhook |
| [System B] | Destination for [output] | API / Scheduled sync |
| [System C] | Notification / routing | Webhook / Email |

---

## Sprint Delivery Model

| Days | Phase | Activities |
|------|-------|-----------|
| 1–2 | **Lock Spec** | Confirm data mapping, edge cases, validation rules, success criteria |
| 3–6 | **Integrate** | Build connections, transformations, error handling, retry logic |
| 7–8 | **Validate** | End-to-end testing, stakeholder UAT, edge case verification |
| 9–10 | **Handoff** | Documentation, runbook, monitoring setup, knowledge transfer |

---

## Outputs

| # | Deliverable | Format |
|---|-------------|--------|
| 1 | Working integration (production-ready) | Deployed to your environment |
| 2 | Data mapping documentation | Markdown / Confluence |
| 3 | Runbook (operations + troubleshooting) | PDF + editable doc |
| 4 | Monitoring + alerting setup | Your observability stack |
| 5 | 30-minute handoff call | Live session |

---

## Boundaries

**Included:**
- Up to [3] system integrations
- Up to [1] workflow end-to-end
- Error handling + retry logic
- Documentation + handoff

**Not included:**
- UI/UX design work
- Data migration (historical backfill)
- Ongoing maintenance (available as retainer)
- New system procurement or licensing

---

## Success Criteria

The sprint is successful when:

1. [ ] [Measurable outcome — e.g., "Weekly readout is generated automatically with <5 min manual review"]
2. [ ] [Measurable outcome — e.g., "Manual copy-paste steps reduced from 12 to 0"]
3. [ ] [Measurable outcome — e.g., "Stakeholders receive updates within 15 minutes of source change"]
4. [ ] All documentation delivered and reviewed
5. [ ] Client team can operate the workflow independently
